meta.title: $50B buyback vs $50B AI moonshot — big-tech capital allocation
meta.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title:
$50B buyback vs $50B
AI moonshot
slides.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title: Pure $50B moonshot is dominated; mirror the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership-first structure instead
slides.action: Execute partnership-first structure: $10-15B equity in a frontier AI lab + $30-35B buyback + $5B adjacent moonshot in a vertical the company's distribution actually moats. Mirror the Microsoft-OpenAI playbook ($13B [G18] stake → $150B+ value creation).

fields.label: CONFIDENCE
fields.value: HIGH that pure $50B moonshot is strictly dominated · MEDIUM-HIGH that pure $50B buyback is dominated in 2026 AI-bifurcated regime · MEDIUM on partnership-first executability (gated by lab availability + antitrust)

fields.label: DEPENDS ON
fields.value: Frontier lab accepts minority non-voting equity without exclusivity [C5:council] · Antitrust clears $10-15B stake · AI is a platform shift · Moonshot base rate 70-85% failure holds · Staged deployment kill criteria board-credible

fields.label: MONITOR
fields.value: Frontier lab partnership announcements by peers · IRA buyback excise tax debate · Meta Reality Labs quarterly burn · Microsoft-OpenAI exclusivity renegotiation · Peer $50B+ moonshots · ML talent attrition · Foundation model benchmarks

fields.label: PRE-MORTEM
fields.value: Exclusivity locks multi-lab optionality (P=0.40) · Antitrust drag 12-18 months (P=0.30) · Partner lab falls behind in capability race (P=0.20) · Activist forces reversal mid-deployment (P=0.25) · Vertical moonshot slips into meta-trap (P=0.30)

fields.label: DEEPEST DISAGREEMENT
fields.value: Optimist: 25% [C2:optimist] AI moonshot platform capture. Pessimist: 5% [C2:pessimist]. Council range 5-25% — partnership-first captures 60-70% of upside without 70-85% [C3:pessimist] downside.

fields.label: REVIEW TRIGGER
fields.value: Frontier lab terms include problematic exclusivity · AI platform shift weakens · Activist 13D on big tech · Meta reverses Reality Labs · Microsoft-OpenAI renegotiation · IRA buyback hike passes

slides.source: Source: Decision Brief (full mode, 2 iterations, council 5/5 endorsement on h6, 12/12 adversarial survival) · [G18], [C5:council], [C9:council], [C10:council]

slides.lead_question: A 5-persona council, 6 competing hypotheses, two iterations to convergence, and 12 adversarial stress scenarios — what survived?

slides.title: Two iterations of a 5-persona council and 12 adversarial scenarios converged on partnership-first structure
bullets: 5 personas spawned as separate subagents — Optimist, Pessimist, Competitor, Regulator, Customer — each with independent context
6 hypotheses generated, ranging from pure buyback (h1) and pure moonshot (h2) to hybrid split (h5) to the synthesis winner (h6 partnership-first, MSFT-OpenAI mirror)
2 iterations — convergence reached at iteration 2 with 85% assumption stability, 1 productive contradiction remaining
Adversary phase generated 12 stress scenarios across worst-cases, black swans, and irrational actors — h6 survives 12/12 [C10:council]
Sensitivity analysis on 8 key assumptions — 4 high-sensitivity, 3 medium, 1 low
Real-world anchor: Microsoft-OpenAI partnership ($13B [G18] stake → $150B+ value creation) is the proven incumbent AI playbook the recommendation mirrors
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief Convergence Log; engine-protocol.md

slides.title: Six hypotheses surfaced; only h6 (partnership-first) survived 5/5 council and 12/12 adversarial scenarios
rows: AI is utility not platform · Market rewards discipline · Mature company can't ship moonshot
AI is platform shift · Mature company can ship · Incumbent distribution advantage
Reality Labs pattern applies · Org rigidity real · No partnership available
Market punishes no narrative · AI-era valuation bifurcation · Talent flight
Market accepts hybrid · Split strategy credible · Underfunded moonshot fails same way
Frontier lab accepts partnership · No exclusivity blocks optionality · Antitrust clears minority stake

slides.title: Plotted on capital at risk × optionality preserved, h6 captures the AI upside without sitting where Reality Labs failed

items.label: h2
items.legend: Pure $50B moonshot — Reality Labs pattern, 70-85% base rate failure [C3:pessimist]

items.label: h3
items.legend: Moonshot meta-trap — risk scenario of h2, organizational rigidity tax 3-5×

items.label: h4
items.legend: Buyback signals no vision — narrative compression in AI-bifurcated market

items.label: h5
items.legend: Hybrid split — pleases neither activist faction, underfunds the moonshot

items.label: h1
items.legend: Pure $50B buyback — mechanical EPS accretion 8-12% [C1:council], capital not at risk

items.label: h6
items.legend: Partnership-first — $10-15B equity + $30-35B buyback + $5B vertical · captures 30-50% [C9:council] of AI upside

slides.source: Source: Synthesis from Decision Brief — bubble size = council_agreement × dominant probability of the hypothesis

slides.lead_question: Where the personas converged, where they diverged, and what the disagreement itself reveals about the decision.

slides.title: The mechanical buyback (P=0.85) and the moonshot burn pattern (P=0.65) are the two highest-confidence signals

effects.label: Buyback captures capital return signal at smaller size [h6]
Balanced signal from hybrid (pleases both narratives)
Adjacent vertical moonshot $5B captures narrow market [h6]

commentary_lines: Top 12 effects ranked by council_agreement × probability
Bar color = council agreement (navy = 5/5, fading to pale = 1/5)
Black bracket = persona disagreement range — width is the uncertainty signal
Two of the three highest-agreement effects (5/5) belong to h6 partnership-first
The Reality Labs burn pattern (P=0.65, 5/5) dominates pure-moonshot risk math
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Effects Map (top 15 by council_agreement × probability) · [C1:council], [C9:council], [C10:council]

slides.title: Optimist's 25% vs Pessimist's 5% on AI-as-platform IS the data — partnership-first dominates either world
left_items: Won on second-order competitive dynamics and the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership-first synthesis
Spotted the structural asymmetry: $50B is HALF of frontier capex per hyperscaler [G4] — pure moonshot starts underfunded
Surfaced the Bing counter-example as analog — 20 years of Microsoft distribution couldn't overcome Google's category lock-in
Authored the alt_partnership_first_moonshot_second variant that synthesized into h6
slides.right_header: KEY DIVIDE: AI PLATFORM SHIFT REALITY
right_items: Optimist: 25% [C2:optimist] AI moonshot category capture probability
Pessimist: 5% [C2:pessimist] AI moonshot category capture probability
Council range 5-25% on the moonshot upside — the disagreement IS the data
Customer was weakest (#5 peer rank) — public-equity decision had more signaling weight than product-side insights
BLIND SPOT: 4/5 personas inflated option-value probabilities on moonshot upside; underfunded-vs-frontier point fixed in h6

slides.title: Competitor topped peer rank on second-order dynamics; Customer's narrow product lens drove its #5 placement
commentary_lines: Each persona scored 1-5 across five rigor dimensions, derived from peer-review and critique outputs
Competitor's structural depth surfaced the partnership-first alternative that synthesized into h6 — Microsoft-OpenAI mirror
Pessimist's adversarial coverage anchored the Reality Labs precedent and 70-85% [C3:pessimist] base rate failure rate
Regulator's quantitative rigor scored highest on antitrust and IRA tax structural workarounds
Customer's narrow lens (#5 peer rank) is structural — public-equity decisions weight signaling over product
slides.source: Source: Synthesized from Decision Brief — Council Dynamics + Critique outputs

slides.title: Twelve adversarial scenarios — h6 survives 12/12; pure moonshot survives only 5/12

items: AI liability suit hits moonshot mid-development (P=0.18)
Stock market correction compresses all multiples (P=0.25)
Activist hostile bid for the company (P=0.10)
Competitor launches $100B buyback + AI partnership same week (P=0.12)
Key AI researcher publicly trashes moonshot on social (P=0.22)

slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Adversarial Scenarios · [C23:adversary] through [C34:adversary]

slides.title: Four high-sensitivity assumptions are load-bearing — if any one fails, partnership-first becomes the staged-hybrid fallback

assumptions.label: AI is a platform shift (not commoditized utility)
Frontier AI lab accepts partnership/minority equity
Antitrust approval for $10-15B frontier lab investment
IRA Section 4501 1% excise tax on buybacks persists

commentary_lines: Bar length = sensitivity weight × number of effects impacted
Dark-blue bar = HIGH sensitivity, lighter = MEDIUM, palest = LOW
Three of four HIGH-sensitivity assumptions are external — gated by frontier labs and regulators
If frontier lab partnership is unavailable (all labs exclusive), h6 collapses to staged-hybrid fallback
Antitrust review timing alone can drag 12-18 months [C40:council] — pre-filing with regulators before announcement is the mitigation
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Key Assumptions · sensitivity per effects-impacted × HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW weight

slides.lead_question: Seven checkpoints from Month 0 board approval to Month 60 review — every gate has a kill criterion before the next commitment.

slides.title: The recommendation maps to a seven-checkpoint roadmap with binding kill criteria at every gate
slides.subtitle: Failure at any gate triggers either reversion to staged-hybrid fallback or capital redeployment
rows: LOI signed with at least one lab
Vertical team can't be hired → skip moonshot
Vertical moonshot tranche 2 ($2.5B) gated on milestones
Partner falls behind; moonshot fails → redeploy capital
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Appendix A: Decision Timeline · binding kill criteria per [C38:council]

Apple $110B buyback May 2024 → stock +7% despite -4% sales, -10% iPhone
Microsoft-OpenAI: $13B stake + exclusivity → $150B+ value creation (the partnership-first proof point)
Moonshot platform capture P=0.25 in optimist view
Moonshot platform capture P=0.05 in pessimist view
Frontier lab partnership availability P=0.5 (range 0.3-0.65) — binary precondition for h6
Partnership captures 30-50% of AI upside without organizational rigidity burden
